_comment: Three MCP servers wired into one Claude config. ida-pro-mcp talks to the IDA plugin on 192.168.131.1:13337 (static analysis). x64dbg-automate-mcp + x64dbg-mcp-fw both talk to the x64dbg-automate plugin on 192.168.131.129 (dynamic analysis). Both upstream plugins bind on their own machines; this config runs the client side wherever Claude lives.

ida-pro-mcp._note: Static analysis. The IDA Pro plugin (ida-pro-mcp) listens on 13337 on the IDA host. ONE-TIME INSTALL on the framework host: `uv tool install git+https://github.com/mrexodia/ida-pro-mcp` — this puts `ida-pro-mcp` on PATH. The older `uvx --from git+...` form re-fetches the repo on every MCP startup and routinely exceeds Claude's 30 s connect timeout. Inside IDA, run `uvx --from git+https://github.com/mrexodia/ida-pro-mcp ida-pro-mcp --install` once to drop the plugin, then enable 'Allow remote connections' in the plugin's settings so it binds 0.0.0.0:13337 instead of localhost. Upstream auto-discovery only works when IDA runs on the same machine as Claude (it scans local lockfiles under ~/.idapro/mcp/instances/); for cross-host setups you must pass --ida-rpc. Verify the endpoint with `nc -vz 192.168.131.1 13337` (Linux) or `Test-NetConnection 192.168.131.1 -Port 13337` (PowerShell).

x64dbg-automate._note: Low-level debugger primitives (~40 tools: read_memory, set_breakpoint, step_into, etc.). Call connect_remote(host='192.168.131.129', req_port=41201, pub_port=41200) at the start of each session.

x64dbg-mcp-fw._note: High-level skills + VM lifecycle (this repo, ~19 tools). Connects to the same x64dbg-automate plugin via short-lived ZMQ sessions.

env._note_host: VM host-only adapter IP (VMnet1). Verify VMnet1 is the host-only network, not NAT.
env._note_ports: Must match the x64dbg Automate Settings dialog. REQ/REP=41201, PUB/SUB=41200.
env._note_vmrun: vmrun tools only work when this MCP server runs on the same machine as VMware Workstation. Omit VMRUN_PATH/VMX_PATH if Claude runs on a third host that can't drive the hypervisor.
